params.text:
Good nutrition and hydration in care

Eating and drinking well is essential to health, healing and wellbeing. Good nutrition and hydration help maintain strength and mobility, support skin integrity and wound healing, reduce infections and falls, and lift mood. Mealtimes are also social occasions that support dignity, choice and enjoyment.

NICE guidance highlights that many people in care settings are at risk of not eating and drinking enough. Care workers are well placed to notice early warning signs and to support people to eat and drink in a way that suits them.

  • Offer regular meals, snacks and drinks throughout the day.
  • Encourage fluids - aim to support people to drink regularly across the day, not just at mealtimes.
  • Make mealtimes unhurried, pleasant and person-centred.

params.question: Supporting someone to drink regularly throughout the day, not only at mealtimes, helps prevent dehydration.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Offering drinks only at mealtimes is often not enough - people should be supported to drink regularly throughout the day.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Fluids should be offered and encouraged across the whole day to help maintain good hydration.
